title: DHS Inspector General Tells Congress DHS Has 'Systematically Obstructed' His Work
excerpt: DHS Inspector General Joseph Cuffari sent a letter to Congress asserting that DHS leadership under Secretary Kristi Noem had 'systematically obstructed' OIG oversight work across 11 investigations, including by revoking access to critical databases such as ICE's Enforcement Integrated Database. Noem publicly denied the allegations.
content:
In early March 2026, DHS Inspector General Joseph Cuffari sent an extraordinary letter to Congress alleging that DHS leadership had obstructed his office's oversight work across 11 separate investigations over several months.

  • Cuffari's letter stated that DHS had 'systematically obstructed' OIG's work, including by revoking OIG's longstanding access to ICE's Enforcement Integrated Database and a database tracking employee and contractor security clearances.
  • DHS General Counsel James Percival had attached a letter warning that any OIG notification to Congress about access concerns would be in 'bad faith and bordering on material misrepresentation.'
  • Cuffari told Congress the obstruction was 'particularly egregious' in a specific pending criminal investigation, where DHS proposed conditions OIG could not accept without risking the integrity of the case.
  • Sen. Tammy Duckworth had previously accused Secretary Noem of intimidating the IG from investigating Border Patrol agent use-of-force incidents by repeatedly reminding OIG of the Secretary's rarely-invoked authority to cancel investigations under 5 U.S.C. § 417.
  • Noem publicly denied blocking investigations, claiming the IG had failed to follow proper procedures by not providing scoping memos. Cuffari's office rejected this characterization.
  • The Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs announced it was investigating Cuffari's allegations.
"While all of the matters listed are important, the Department's obstruction is particularly egregious in a specific pending criminal investigation," Cuffari wrote to Congress.
